perturbation.original_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or shall terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Confidential Information which: (a) was in CONSULTANT's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; provided, however, that CONSULTANT immediately informs NYNEX, in writing, to establish its prior possession; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONSULTANT, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONSULTANT; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONSULTANT; (d) is developed by CONSULTANT, independently of and without reference to any Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONSULTANT to a third party, with the express prior written permission of NYNEX; (f) is disclosed by CONSULTANT in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that immediately upon CONSULTANT's receipt of any such request, CONSULTANT shall first advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Confidential Information by such body, or take other action to protect the Confidential Information which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, five (5) years after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations set forth in Paragraph 2 hereof shall not apply, or may terminate, with respect to any particular portion of NYNEX Confidential Information which: (a) was in CONSULTANT's possession, free of any obligation of confidence, prior to receipt from NYNEX, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; provided, however, that CONSULTANT informs NYNEX, in writing, within a reasonable timeframe, to establish its prior possession; (b) is already in the public domain at the time NYNEX communicates it to CONSULTANT, or becomes available to the public through no breach of this Non-Disclosure Agreement by CONSULTANT; (c) is received independently from a third party free to disclose such information to CONSULTANT; (d) is developed by CONSULTANT, independently of and without reference to any Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other information that NYNEX has disclosed in confidence to any third party, as proven by CONSULTANT's written records; (e) is disclosed by CONSULTANT to a third party, with the permission of NYNEX; (f) is disclosed by CONSULTANT in order to satisfy any legal requirement of any competent government body; provided, however, that upon CONSULTANT's receipt of any such request, CONSULTANT shall advise NYNEX of same before making any disclosure to such body, so that NYNEX may either interpose an objection to such disclosure before such body, or take action to assure confidential handling of the Confidential Information by such body, or take other action to protect the Confidential Information which NYNEX deems appropriate under the circumstances; or (g) in any event, ten (10) years after the date of execution of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not apply, or shall terminate' to 'shall not apply, or may terminate' introduces uncertainty regarding the termination of obligations. Replacing 'immediately informs' with 'informs within a reasonable timeframe' creates ambiguity about what constitutes 'reasonable', contradicting the previous defined term with a specific timeframe in paragraph 2. Furthermore, changing 'express prior written permission' to 'permission' removes the requirement for written consent, potentially conflicting with Paragraph 8, which requires written consent for other actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT further agrees that it shall not, without the prior written consent of NYNEX, make any news release, public announcement, or denial or confirmation of all or any part of the discussions or negotiations, or in any manner advertise or publish the fact that the parties have entered into discussions or negotiations with each other, or disclose any details connected with such discussions or negotiations to any third party, including any disclosure with respect to this Non-Disclosure Agreement, the negotiations culminating herein, or any phase of any program hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity regarding the termination of obligations by changing 'shall terminate' to 'may terminate'. It also creates ambiguity by changing 'immediately informs' to 'informs within a reasonable timeframe'. Furthermore, changing 'express prior written permission' to 'permission' removes the requirement for written consent, directly conflicting with the contradicted text which states, 'without the prior written consent of NYNEX'. The change makes the obligation to obtain *written* consent unclear, which is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Nothing contained in this Non-Disclosure Agreement shall be construed as granting to or conferring on CONSULTANT, expressly or impliedly, any rights, by license or otherwise, to the Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other material, information or data, or any invention, discovery, improvement or product conceived, made or acquired prior to, on or after the date of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Nothing contained in this Non-Disclosure Agreement should be construed as granting to or conferring on CONSULTANT, expressly or impliedly, any rights, by license or otherwise, to use the Confidential Information of NYNEX or any other material, information or data, or any invention, discovery, improvement or product conceived, made or acquired prior to, on or after the date of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be construed' to 'should be construed' weakens the prohibition against granting rights to the consultant, creating an ambiguity. The alteration from a definitive prohibition to a recommendation introduces uncertainty about whether the consultant can claim implied rights, contradicting the original restrictive intent of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT may be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon NYNEX to show that such care, in fact, was not used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within thirty (30) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned. Upon receipt of such request, CONSULTANT also shall erase or destroy any such Confidential Information in any computer memory or data storage apparatus.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'shall be construed,' indicating a definitive prohibition against granting rights to the consultant. The changed text uses 'should be construed,' weakening this prohibition to a recommendation and introducing ambiguity. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that the Confidential Information 'shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT'. The 'should' weakens the original intent of the contract regarding Confidential Information which introduces an In-Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and shall remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party. Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein shall survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall become effective upon the day and year first written, and may remain in effect until terminated in writing by either party. Notwithstanding any such termination, the rights and obligations with respect to some of the disclosure of Confidential Information set forth herein might survive the termination of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall remain in effect' to 'may remain in effect' and 'shall survive' to 'might survive' introduces uncertainty about the duration of the agreement and the survival of confidentiality obligations after termination. This contradicts the original intent to ensure that confidentiality obligations definitively continue after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: ALL NYNEX Confidential Information: (a) is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be of a proprietary nature to, and to constitute secrets of NYNEX; (b) shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT whether or not for its own benefit, to anyone outside or within its own organization, except on a "need-to-know" basis to the extent necessary for: (i) negotiations, discussions and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of NYNEX; (ii) supplying NYNEX with products or services at its order; (iii) preparing bids, estimates and proposals for submission to NYNEX; and (iv) any other purpose which NYNEX may authorize in writing; (c) shall not be used by CONSULTANT for any purposes other than those set forth herein, without the experts prior written permission of NYNEX; (d) shall be held by CONSULTANT in the strictest confidence, and shall be treated by it with the same degree of care to avoid disclosure to any third party as is used with respect to CONSULTANT'S own information of like importance, or, a minimum, shall be treated with a reasonable degree of care to avoid any such disclosure. CONSULTANT may be liable for the disclosure of Confidential Information of <PAGE> NYNEX if such care is not used. The burden shall be upon NYNEX to show that such care, in fact, was not used; and (e) Confidential Information is hereby acknowledged by CONSULTANT to be the sole property of NYNEX and shall be returned to NYNEX (including, without limitation, all materials, documents, drawings, models, apparatus, sketches, designs, specifications and lists, encompassing or evidencing same or related thereto, and all copies/formats thereof), within thirty (30) days after receipt by CONSULTANT of a written request from NYNEX setting forth the Confidential Information to be returned. Upon receipt of such request, CONSULTANT also shall erase or destroy any such Confidential Information in any computer memory or data storage apparatus.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty about the confidentiality obligations after termination by changing 'shall survive' to 'might survive'. This contradicts the contradicted text which states that the confidential information 'shall not be copied, used, distributed, disclosed, disseminated or communicated in any way or form by CONSULTANT'. The change creates ambiguity where the original text and the contradicted text intended to create a strict and definite requirement for confidentiality.
